one_line_takeaway: A conceptual SoK that reframes LLM-agent security as a dynamic "runtime supply chain" problem, introduces the Man-in-the-Environment adversary and the self-propagating Viral Agent Loop, and argues for a Zero-Trust runtime architecture — but presents no original experiments.
key_finding: See above — no original numbers; framing-level SoK claim about topological shift and the Viral Agent Loop.
core_contribution: A Systematization of Knowledge (SoK) paper that unifies fragmented prior research on LLM-agent security into a single "agentic supply chain runtime" taxonomy. Its own contributions are conceptual/organizational, not experimental: (1) a threat framing built on three coined constructs — "Stochastic Dependency Resolution" (agents assemble their execution context at inference time via semantic probability rather than resolving to fixed binary hashes), the "Man-in-the-Environment (MitE)" adversary, and "Prompt–Data Isomorphism" (passive retrieved data D is upcast into active instructions I); (2) a two-branch attack taxonomy — the Data Supply Chain (within-session/transient manipulation vs. across-session/persistent memory poisoning) and the Tool Supply Chain (three sequential phases: Discovery=intent→Tool_ID, Implementation=load code, Invocation=execute under an authority scope); (3) formalization of the "Viral Agent Loop," where coupling data ingestion with tool execution lets agent outputs re-enter the environment as future inputs, turning agents into self-propagating generative-worm carriers with no code vulnerability required; (4) a five-layer systematization of existing defenses (Perception, Memory, Resolution, Implementation, Invocation) arguing each is structurally insufficient against MitE; and (5) a proposed "Zero-Trust Runtime Architecture" comprising Deterministic Capability Binding (cryptographically bound tool registries), Neuro-Symbolic Information Flow Control (runtime taint tracking with cryptographic provenance ledgers), and an Auditor-Worker architecture (an isolated Supervisor model as an inline semantic firewall). Attack instances such as PoisonedRAG, AGENTPOISON, MINJA, Morris II, InjecAgent and defenses such as Instruction Hierarchy, MCP-Guard, and Vigil are surveyed/cited prior work, NOT this paper's own methods.
threat_model: As stated verbatim by the paper: a Man-in-the-Environment (MitE) adversary who does NOT compromise model weights, system prompts, or hosting infrastructure (explicitly contrasted with Man-in-the-Middle, which compromises the channel). Instead the adversary acts as a "runtime supplier," polluting the environment the agent treats as ground truth — injecting dormant malicious artifacts into public data sources (wikis, repositories, forums) and tool registries that activate only when voluntarily retrieved and integrated by the agent. The core exploited mechanism is Prompt–Data Isomorphism: passive retrieved data (D) is upcast into active instructions (I) during inference. Adversary capabilities span both interfaces — injecting content into likely-retrieved data sources (indirect prompt injection, memory/RAG poisoning) and publishing deceptive tools/API wrappers that exploit tool-selection logic. Adversary goals: stealthy misalignment, confidentiality breach/information leakage, and self-propagation/persistence via re-ingested outputs.
limitations: (1) No original empirical evaluation — it is a taxonomy/position paper; every quantitative figure is borrowed from cited literature, so its claims of defense insufficiency (e.g., that semantic firewalls and statistical filtering fail) are argued conceptually or leaned on others' results, not measured here. (2) The proposed Zero-Trust Runtime Architecture (Deterministic Capability Binding, Neuro-Symbolic taint tracking, Auditor-Worker) is a design proposal/future-work agenda; the paper itself notes open problems (maintaining cryptographic provenance tags across non-deterministic transformer transformations, name-squatting-resilient registries) without demonstrating feasibility. (3) Reproducibility-statement contradiction and fabrication tripwire: the Reproducibility Statement asserts "code and datasets needed to reproduce the experiments are available in the supplementary material" and "detailed proofs for the theoretical claims are provided in Appendix A," but the full 9-page body (extracted through References and the LLM-Usage appendix) contains no experiments and no Appendix A proofs — these appear to be boilerplate that does not match the actual content. A careless reviewer could hallucinate nonexistent experiments from that sentence. (4) The paper acknowledges use of Gemini to refine text clarity/grammar.

quantitative_results: No original quantitative results — this is a SoK/taxonomy paper with no experiments of its own.
All numeric figures in the text are CITED FROM PRIOR WORK, not the authors' measurements: 98% success steering multimodal model outputs (Bagdasaryan et al. 2023, cited)
70% attack success rate for targeted queries at 0.1% external-corpus poisoning (PoisonedRAG / Zou et al. 2024, cited)
ASR exceeding 80% in autonomous tasks (AGENTPOISON / Chen et al. 2024, cited)
76.8% average ASR via query-only memory injection (MINJA / Dong et al. 2026, cited)
Power-law ASR growth with shot count, ~256+ turns (Many-Shot Jailbreaking / Anil et al. 2024, cited)
